message:
refactor(database): restructure DbPool::get() to validate outside lock

Restructures the connection acquisition path in DbPool::get() to
minimize lock hold time and prevent potential deadlocks during
connection validation and disconnection:

Previous design: held the pool mutex continuously while iterating
idle connections, validating them (via manager.is_valid), and
disconnecting stale ones (via manager.disconnect).  If either
callback attempted to interact with the pool or any shared state
protected by the same lock, it would deadlock.

New design: uses a loop that:
1. Acquires the lock briefly to pop one idle connection (or allocate
   a slot if none available)
2. Releases the lock before calling manager.disconnect on expired/
   stale connections or manager.is_valid for checkout validation
3. On validation failure, re-acquires the lock only to decrement
   the total count, then continues the loop

This ensures that all potentially blocking manager callbacks
(disconnect, is_valid, connect) execute without holding the pool
mutex, matching the same lock-narrowing pattern applied to
return_connection and discard_connection in a prior commit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
